title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot speaks with Mrs. Goodbody, who clarifies that while she uses a crystal ball for seeing, she does not use a Ouija board. Mrs. Goodbody discusses her belief in people who are "born" evil, citing a cruel girl she knew in Yorkshire. Regarding the Reynolds family, she describes Mr. Reynolds as unremarkable and Mrs. Reynolds as anxious. She characterizes the daughter, Ann, as smart but self-satisfied, and the other daughter, Joyce, as a frequent liar who tries too hard to appear intelligent. She describes the son, Leo, as a highly intelligent boy interested in physics and math, but warns he is a trickster who likely uses secrets to extort hush money from others. When Poirot asks about a foreign girl who supposedly ran away, Mrs. Goodbody implies she will not get far.

Later, Mrs. Oliver is approached by Mrs. Liman, a cleaning lady who previously worked for Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. Mrs. Liman seeks advice regarding a legal matter from the past involving a codicil to Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith's will. Mrs. Liman explains that the codicil left all the money to the foreign girl who cared for Mrs. Llewellyn-Smith. This caused suspicion among relatives, specifically Mrs. Drake, who wanted to invalidate the document. Mrs. Liman notes that lawyers suggested the foreign girl might have written the codicil herself.
